description:
h2. Epic Goal
*As a* data controller / data subject,
*I want* CRANIS2 to be GDPR-compliant for its own processing,
*so that* users have a real privacy policy, terms, data-export and deletion rights, and the controller is correctly identified.

h3. Scope
Audit-derived Privacy Policy + ToS (Beta); essential-only cookie assessment (no banner); dynamic /docs/:slug + footer links; data export + account deletion + retention cleanup endpoints; personal-ownership rewrite (Andi as controller, CNIL lead authority).

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/account.test.ts}} + {{routes/docs.test.ts}}, *then* export/delete + slug routing pass (27 GDPR tests).
* *Given* the policies, *then* they name Andi personally as controller and CNIL as lead authority.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-333…CRAN-338

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* data subject,
*I want* a GDPR-compliant Privacy Policy from a real data audit,
*so that* I can see exactly what PII is processed, by whom, and for how long.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/PRIVACY-POLICY.md}}, *then* it carries the audit-derived inventory.
* *Given* the frontend, *then* it renders at {{/docs/privacy-policy}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
Covers PII categories, sub-processors (Stripe/Resend/Anthropic/Git), retention, DSR rights; Beta-flagged.

h3. Sources
Session 57 GDPR commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* user,
*I want* Terms of Service with beta + AI-limitation disclaimers,
*so that* the legal terms of use are clear before I rely on the platform.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/TERMS-OF-SERVICE.md}}, *then* it's at HEAD and served at {{/docs/terms-of-service}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
Liability cap; governing law England & Wales.

h3. Sources
Session 57 GDPR commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* data subject,
*I want* confirmation that only essential storage is used,
*so that* no cookie-consent banner is needed and I know what's stored.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the Privacy Policy, *then* it lists exactly two storage items (JWT + help-panel width).

h3. Implementation Notes
Essential-only per GDPR/ePrivacy.

h3. Sources
Session 57 GDPR commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* user,
*I want* dynamic doc routing and footer legal links,
*so that* I can reach any doc by slug and find the legal links everywhere.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/docs.test.ts}}, *then* dynamic slug routing is covered.
* *Given* login/signup/accept-invite/landing/docs, *then* footer links are visible.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{/docs/:slug}}; {{doc_pages}} seeds privacy-policy + terms-of-service.

h3. Sources
Session 57 GDPR commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* data subject,
*I want* data export, account deletion and retention cleanup,
*so that* I can exercise my GDPR Art. 15/17/20 rights and data isn't kept forever.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/account.test.ts}}, *then* export + delete are covered (27 tests).
* *Given* {{routes/admin/data-retention.ts}}, *then* the cleanup job is exposed.

h3. Implementation Notes
Art. 15/20 export; Art. 17 deletion; Art. 5(1)(e) retention cleanup.

h3. Sources
Session 57 GDPR commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* data controller,
*I want* the policies rewritten to name the real controller,
*so that* the controller/operator is correctly identified as an individual with the right supervisory authority.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the Privacy Policy + ToS, *then* both name Andi personally (La Vallée, 50150 Sourdeval, France) as controller and CNIL as lead authority.

h3. Implementation Notes
Generic GDPR (no UK GDPR); governing law England & Wales for future UK NewCo.

h3. Sources
Session 63 personal-ownership rewrite commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* platform owner / affiliate,
*I want* an affiliate programme with bonus codes, ledger, statements and a self-service dashboard,
*so that* affiliates drive signups via discount codes and can see their own earnings, while I manage the ledger centrally.

h3. Scope
Schema + signup bonus-code integration; admin affiliates page + ledger; monthly statement automation; self-service affiliate dashboard; 35 Vitest tests.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{admin-affiliates}} + {{affiliate}} + {{auth-bonus-code}} + {{bonus-code}} tests, *then* 35 pass.
* *Given* a validated code at signup, *then* it applies a discount and {{users.canonicalBonusCode}} is set.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-339…CRAN-343

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* new user with a code,
*I want* bonus-code validation wired into signup,
*so that* a valid affiliate code applies a discount at signup.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{auth-bonus-code.test.ts}} + {{bonus-code.test.ts}}, *then* they pass.
* *Given* a used code, *then* {{users.canonicalBonusCode}} is populated.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{bonus_codes}}/{{affiliate_ledger}}/{{affiliates}}; canonicalisation (lowercased, dashes kept).

h3. Sources
Session 60 Phase 1 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform admin,
*I want* an admin affiliates page with ledger management,
*so that* I can manage affiliates and their ledgers centrally.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/admin-affiliates.test.ts}}, *then* it passes.

h3. Implementation Notes
389-line router; list/create/edit; credit/debit/payout; on-demand statements.

h3. Sources
Session 60 Phase 2 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* affiliate,
*I want* automated monthly statements,
*so that* I receive a monthly statement of my earnings without asking.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the statement template, *then* it renders ledger summary + balance + payout history.

h3. Implementation Notes
312-line service; runs 1st of month for the prior month.

h3. Sources
Session 60 Phase 3 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* affiliate,
*I want* a self-service dashboard for my codes and earnings,
*so that* I can see my own bonus codes, ledger and balance.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{routes/affiliate.test.ts}}, *then* it passes.
* *Given* an affiliate-flagged user, *then* the sidebar shows the Affiliate section.

h3. Implementation Notes
311-line route + 392-line page.

h3. Sources
Session 60 Phase 4 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* engineer,
*I want* full test coverage of the affiliate programme,
*so that* the programme is verified end-to-end.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the 4 test files, *then* 35 tests pass.

h3. Implementation Notes
admin-affiliates / affiliate / auth-bonus-code / bonus-code.

h3. Sources
Session 60 Phase 5 commits.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* platform owner,
*I want* CRANIS2 live in production on cranis2.com,
*so that* real customers can sign up on an EU-sovereign, TLS-secured, backed-up production deployment.

h3. Scope
Infomaniak VPS + domain; host NGINX + Let's Encrypt auto-renew; Docker stack deploy + prod env; localhost-bound app containers; GFS backup + age-encrypted dev mirror; /welcome→/getting-started; affiliate schema patch (with debt #100); prod smoke test; outstanding user-side tasks.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{https://cranis2.com}}, *then* it returns 200 with a valid LE cert (auto-renew active).
* *Given* a prod smoke test, *then* signup→verify→/getting-started works end-to-end.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-344…CRAN-351 (+ user-side outstanding tracked at CRAN-377)

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* platform owner,
*I want* an EU VPS and the cranis2.com domain,
*so that* production runs on EU-sovereign infrastructure on the real domain.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{dig cranis2.com}}, *then* it resolves to the VPS IP.

h3. Implementation Notes
Infomaniak VPS; DNS pointed.

h3. Sources
Session 62 launch commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform owner,
*I want* host NGINX with auto-renewing Let's Encrypt TLS,
*so that* the site is HTTPS with certs that renew themselves.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{https://cranis2.com}}, *then* 200 + valid LE cert.
* *Given* {{systemctl list-timers}}, *then* the renew timer is active.

h3. Implementation Notes
Host NGINX terminates TLS, proxies into the Docker stack.

h3. Sources
Session 62 launch commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform owner,
*I want* the Docker stack deployed with production env config,
*so that* prod runs the full stack with correct settings.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docker compose ps}}, *then* all services run on prod.
* *Given* {{.env}}, *then* it has the documented prod values.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{FRONTEND_URL=https://cranis2.com}}, {{LOG_LEVEL=info}}, {{DEV_SKIP_EMAIL=false}}, {{EMAIL_FROM=info@poste.cranis2.com}}.

h3. Sources
Session 62 launch commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* security engineer,
*I want* app containers bound to localhost in prod (replayed to dev),
*so that* only NGINX is publicly reachable, and dev compose matches prod.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docker-compose.yml}}, *then* {{127.0.0.1:3001:3001}} + {{127.0.0.1:3004:3004}}.

h3. Implementation Notes
Applied on prod at launch; replayed to dev via {{20980de}}.

h3. Sources
Commit {{20980de}} (2026-05-07 01:42).

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* GFS prod backups with an age-encrypted dev mirror,
*so that* prod is retained on a GFS schedule and dev holds an encrypted offsite copy.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{docs/backup-retention.md}}, *then* it documents the full scheme.
* *Given* dev cron, *then* a 03:00 UTC age-pull runs.

h3. Implementation Notes
7d/4w/12m via WS1; age key escrowed on a keyring USB.

h3. Sources
Session 62 backup-arch commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* new user,
*I want* the post-signup landing renamed to /getting-started (replayed to dev),
*so that* the landing route reads clearly and dev/prod converge.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the 7 frontend files, *then* they use {{/getting-started}}.
* *Given* {{routes/auth.ts}}, *then* it redirects there after verification.

h3. Implementation Notes
Applied on prod; brought back into git via {{ede802f}}.

h3. Sources
Commit {{ede802f}} (2026-05-07 01:41).

h3. User Story
*As a* platform operator,
*I want* the affiliate schema applied to prod on launch day,
*so that* the affiliate tables exist in prod (with the back-port to initDb tracked as debt).

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the remediation, *then* affiliate tables are added to {{pool.ts initDb()}} with idempotent guards (tracked as #100/CRAN-401).

h3. Implementation Notes
Direct prod DDL because initDb lacked the tables; motivated CLAUDE.md rule 13 + SPIKE-03.

h3. Sources
Session 62 launch notes; scratch.md #100.

h3. User Story
*As a* platform owner,
*I want* a production smoke test of the core flow,
*so that* I've confirmed signup→verify→getting-started works live.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a manual prod smoke test, *then* a verified user lands on {{/getting-started}}.

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
Session 62 close notes.

h2. Epic Goal
*As a* sole owner of CRANIS2,
*I want* the codebase cleaned of corporate attribution and correctly licensed to me personally,
*so that* the IP, licensing, copyright and provenance all reflect personal ownership, ready for due diligence or a future NewCo.

h3. Scope
Proprietary LICENSE + SPDX; idempotent licence-header sweep (757 files); 2023-2026 copyright range; email standardisation; entity scrub (Gibbs/Loman/Cavendish); R&D-tax framing stripped + reframed; evidence locker; memory hygiene + rebuild prep; INVESTOR-PITCH drop.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a repo grep, *then* no corporate-entity / HMRC / © 2026 references remain (excluding evidence/scratch).
* *Given* the LICENSE + headers, *then* re-running the sweep is a zero-diff no-op.

h3. Child Stories
* CRAN-353…CRAN-360 + CRAN-368

_As-built (Done). Story points + effort aggregate from child stories._

h3. User Story
*As a* owner,
*I want* a proprietary LICENSE with an SPDX identifier,
*so that* the licensing terms are explicit and machine-identifiable.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* repo root, *then* {{LICENSE}} defines {{LicenseRef-Cranis2-Proprietary}} (Andi MCBURNIE, England & Wales).

h3. Implementation Notes


h3. Sources
Session 63 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* owner,
*I want* an idempotent licence-header sweep across the codebase,
*so that* every in-scope file carries the proprietary header without churn on re-run.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* a re-run, *then* zero diff.
* *Given* the 25 scripts, *then* they retain 0755.

h3. Implementation Notes
757 files headed; 8 package.json get {{license}}+{{author}}; permission-preservation bug fixed.

h3. Sources
Session 63 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* owner,
*I want* the copyright range set to 2023-2026,
*so that* copyright reflects the August-2023 ideation.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{grep -r "© 2026 "}} (excl. evidence/scratch), *then* zero matches at HEAD.

h3. Implementation Notes
Anchored to the FTI/SNRG NIST assessment (June 2023).

h3. Sources
Session 63 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* owner,
*I want* the contact email standardised,
*so that* one canonical contact email across all artefacts.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{grep -r "andi.mcburnie@gmail.com"}} (excl. evidence), *then* only acceptable refs (.env/git config) remain.

h3. Implementation Notes
{{andi@mcburnie.com}}; author field in all 8 package.json.

h3. Sources
Session 63 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* owner,
*I want* every corporate-attribution reference removed,
*so that* no third-party entity is attributed anywhere in the repo.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{grep -r "Loman|Cavendish|Gibbs"}} (excl. evidence/node_modules), *then* zero matches at HEAD.

h3. Implementation Notes
15 files; AI-coder framework rewritten to personal framing; loman-cavendish-capabilities→personal-capabilities; 99-succession rewritten for executors/heirs.

h3. Sources
Session 63 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* owner,
*I want* the R&D-Tax-Relief framing stripped and reframed,
*so that* tax framing that doesn't apply to an individual is gone, the underlying value preserved.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{grep -r "HMRC"}} (excl. evidence/scratch), *then* zero matches at HEAD.

h3. Implementation Notes
Removed from STANDARDS.md + SESSION-TEMPLATE.md; reframed as IP-provenance / audit / due-diligence value.

h3. Sources
Session 63 commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* owner,
*I want* an evidence locker with binaries kept local-only,
*so that* provenance evidence is indexed but sensitive binaries never get pushed.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* {{evidence/README.md}}, *then* it indexes Entry #1 (FTI/SNRG NIST assessment) + Entry #2 (engagement analysis).
* *Given* {{evidence/.gitignore}}, *then* binaries stay local-only.

h3. Implementation Notes
Entry #1 PDF SHA-256 recorded; held on the dev Mac Mini, never pushed.

h3. Sources
Session 63 commits + {{fb39bb0}}.

h3. User Story
*As a* owner / future maintainer,
*I want* memory hygiene and rebuild preparation,
*so that* future sessions never re-introduce corporate framing and the MBP rebuild target is ready.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* the memory dir, *then* {{project_ownership.md}}, {{seis_strategy.md}}, {{rebuild_plan.md}} are present and {{user_profile.md}} is scrubbed.
* *Given* {{ssh mbp}}, *then* the MacBook Pro is reachable.

h3. Implementation Notes
MBP 10.0.0.117 (Ubuntu 24.04, Docker 29.4); /home/andi/cranis2 + 4xxx ports reserved; Forgejo primary, Codeberg mirror.

h3. Sources
Session 63 memory + rebuild-prep commits.

h3. User Story
*As a* owner,
*I want* the superseded investor pitch removed,
*so that* stale positioning docs don't linger in the tree.

h3. Acceptance Criteria
* *Given* HEAD, *then* {{docs/INVESTOR-PITCH.md}} does not exist and {{MARKET-PITCH.md}} is canonical.

h3. Implementation Notes
Staged a long-pending unstaged deletion.

h3. Sources
Commit {{801f127}} (2026-05-07 07:44).
